mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested interaction specifications.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of restrictive policy rates crossing above 4.0% and unemployment crossing above 4.0% indicates a policy-induced economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% AND UNRATE crosses above 4.0%
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 levels over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal is completely subsumed by higher-frequency indicators.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either component moves independently, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels crossing above 320 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 320, or the threshold signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.
